feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I want my 26 hours back.What a shitty sequel. The first game is half as short, i…
entry.content: I want my 26 hours back.What a shitty sequel. The first game is half as short, it's simple, sweet novelty fun. The second game barely improves over the 1st game in gameplay department so ends up being too long for how shallow the gameplay mechanics are.

entry.title: /int/ International: I feel inferior to white people. They are much better looking than my ethnicity …
entry.content: I feel inferior to white people. They are much better looking than my ethnicity and steal our women. They have diverse hair and eye colors. Also, they invented and discovered most things about the modern world. It’s crazy being a STEMcel reading a textbook and almost every name is a white person. Their art/music/architecture is clearly the most sophisticated too. Does this feeling exist in your country?

entry.title: /b/ Random: Ten Commandments 1. I am the Lord your God; you shall not have strange gods befo…
entry.content: Ten Commandments 1. I am the Lord your God; you shall not have strange gods before me. 2. You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain. 3. Remember to keep holy the Lord's Day. 4. Honor your father and your mother. 5. You shall not kill. 6. You shall not commit adultery. 7. You shall not steal. 8. You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor. 9. You shall not covet your neighbor's wife. 10. You shall not covet your neighbor's goods. Beatitudes. Blessed are the poor in spiri

entry.title: /int/ International: >Colombia boasts the highest bird species diversity in the world, with nearly 2,…
entry.content: >Colombia boasts the highest bird species diversity in the world, with nearly 2,000 recorded species representing almost 20% of all avian life on Earth. This unparalleled abundance is driven by the country's varied microclimates—ranging from Andean cloud forests to the Amazon—and isolation from historical geographic barriers. Why don't you visit Colombia and go birdwatching?

entry.content: >back in january >start talking to 36 y.o. lawyer on dating app >eventually agree to meet for coffee >she's very giggly and charming IRL >we chat for a while, the worst I do is mention an interest in mongolian history and wanting to learn horse archery >she's very educated and intelligent obviously >I walk her to her car, slightly lean in for a kiss, she slightly leans away >nothing too awkward though, didn't feel that hot wash of embarasment you get if you really fucked up >later she sends me a

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: In England, many towns and cities end with the suffix "-bury": which comes from …
entry.content: In England, many towns and cities end with the suffix "-bury": which comes from the Old English "burh", or "burg" meaning fortification or fortified settlement. The first of these Anglo-Saxon burhs were built in the 9th century on the orders of Alfred the Great, following the Viking invasion of the north of England. Many burhs were constructed upon pre-existing Roman fortifications, whilst some were constructed completely anew. His successors continued the construction of burhs and they played a

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Have you considered living without home internet and without a phone? I don't ow…
entry.content: Have you considered living without home internet and without a phone? I don't own a house, instead I own 2 apartments in the same building. 1 larger as a home and 1 smaller as an office. So in principle I could just cancel home internet and use the internet only for work or serious research in my office. Then I'd keep my home completely cut off from communication technology. No tv, no phone, no internet. This way I could focus more on reading books, expanding my mind and breaking free from my in

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Free work
entry.content: What kinda freework do you/would you like to do? I recently got into guerilla gardening magick mushrooms in my local parks. I make a few patches for myself and to give to friends, then i make public patches for strangers to find. Recently ive been trying to get familiar with native wildflowers and shrubs to plant around or near my mushroom patches to promote firefly & dragonfly habitat, and to also use their flowering stages as indicators for when my patches start fruiting. It pretty much cost m

entry.title: /g/ Technology: clock thread. functioning clocks are bloated garbage for iCucks. I've recently s…
entry.content: clock thread. functioning clocks are bloated garbage for iCucks. I've recently switched to broken clocks. broken clocks are always right twice a day, while functioning ones are always just a little bit off, meaning they're never right. this allowed me to debloat my time management and schedule aswell, since I only recognize 2 different times per day. I can also manually set the time, just like on a functioning clock, but without all the extra bloat of needing a power source and needing to make s

entry.title: /b/ Random: i have never been more checked out of reality than i am today...i woke up from a…
entry.content: i have never been more checked out of reality than i am today...i woke up from a nap today and my suffering was different...i really do feel like "nobody" right now...my internal truths are becoming obscured...soon, there will be nothing left of the old self that i dragged kicking and screaming up to this point...the pain won't go away, but the memories will fade, and all i will have left is dreams...

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Firefox is degrading
entry.content: Hey in case you haven't noticed, this piece of shit has had a massive memory leak for at least a few months, and I think Pozilla legitimately doesn't have the technical knowhow anymore to fix it. This affects downstream forks like Librewolf btw. NOTHING is being done about this. If I leave it open for a few hours it inevitably consumes my entire 32 gigs of RAM. AI slop garbage code is slowly making all software unusable and and unfixable I'm gonna fucking kill myself.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Sentient Ragdolls?
entry.content: a few weeks ago i made this "Balance" system in roblox similar to gta 4s euphoria. there were 3 versions Version 1 did not work. Version 2 DID work BUT its arms were stuck in a t pose and version 3 had the arms freely move but not have any collision. When the player died they would stand still not falling over as a ragdoll.. there was a ragdoll script that was serverside and a localscript for the balance that was client side. it would cause the player to continue standing by adjusting its trajec

entry.title: /g/ Technology: On this 250th birthday of the greatest nation on the face of the Earth, let's ta…
entry.content: On this 250th birthday of the greatest nation on the face of the Earth, let's take a moment to look at just some of the advancements in computers that came from the USA. >The ENIAC (1st programmable, general purpose computer) >The Transistor >The Intel 4004 (first microprocessor) >Open Source >Free Software / GPL >Fortran, Cobol, Java, Javascript, C, BASIC >UNIX >The GUI >The Mouse >The Internet >The Spreadsheet >The WYSIWYG Word Processor >Hypertext and "The Web" >Email >Wolfenstein 3D & Doom >

entry.title: /g/ Technology: tired of documents rotting
entry.content: Been chipping away at this for a while: I got tired of documents either rotting (links die, apps get abandoned, viewer stops being maintained) or being bloated (PDF needs a whole rendering engine just to show text, Word needs Word). So I made EPP. It's plain text. If your text editor still exists, your document still exists. That's basically the whole pitch. >no binary blobs baked in, stays diffable/greppable >no external links to rot, it's self-contained >works fully offline, no server no runti

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: THE MANDARDS
entry.content: If you can't butcher a hog, you're not a man. (Nutritional sovereignty) If you can't change your own oil, you're not a man. (Mechanical independence) If you can't do your own plumbing, you're not a man. (System mastery) If you can't build and frame your own cabin or house, you're not a man. (Structural creation) If you don't have a truck and trailer, you're not a man. (Logistical muscle) If you can't walk away from your paycheck tomorrow, you're not a man. (Financial leverage) Remember you're a

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Discs vs Downloads
entry.content: Do you prefer digital downloads for games, or physical discs? Pic related, it seems quite a lot of people want a choice. I like discs because you can resell them, and you can buy second-hand discs for less money than brand new games. I guess that's why game companies want to eliminate discs. They don't want you buying second-hand games because it means they lose a sale.
